rss.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DR Congo an international emergency
rss.snippet: The agency added that the outbreak, with around 246 cases and 80 deaths, does not meet the criteria of pandemic emergency.

rss.title: How worrying is the Ebola outbreak in DR Congo?
rss.snippet: An outbreak in DR Congo has been declared a health emergency, with almost 250 suspected cases reported.

rss.title: Large-scale Ukrainian drone attack kills three in Moscow region, says Russia
rss.snippet: Ukraine's president says the strikes are a "justified" response to deadly Russian attacks on Ukrainian cities.

youtube_items.title: [YT] Wealth Inequality in America Has Never Been Worse. The Middle Class Has Disappeared
youtube_items.summary: most people have no clue about how severe wealth inequality has become in America and how much worse it's getting there's a YouTube video by user paulit Zane where he references a study done by a Harvard Economist and professor and the study asked 5 000 Americans how they believed wealth was distributed in the United States the group was first asked what they thought the wealth distribution in America looked like dividing up American Wealth into five segments the participants came up with the following a bar chart that looks like this with the top 20 owning a little bit more than half of the nation's wealth and the bottom 20 owning just a sliver of wealth no more than five percent participants were then asked what they thought the ideal distribution should be over 9 out of 10 participants

youtube_items.title: [YT] How Hong Kong became one of the world’s most unequal cities
youtube_items.summary: It's a city where some people live in the lap of luxury while others call metal cages home. Hong Kong is a paradise for for those rich and is a hell for those uh poor. They say this Hong Kong's richest earn 81 times more than its poorest residents. According to median figures released in a report by the charity Oxfam, that's 130,000 Hong Kong dollars or roughly 16,560 per month for people on the wealthiest end of the scale, but a mere $1,600 Hong Kong dollars or $23 for the poorest. And the problem is worsening. Hong Kong's wealth cap had more than doubled by the first quarter of 2024 compared to data from 5 years earlier. Income inequality is a major challenge for many cities around the world. But in Hong Kong, the disparity has reached staggering levels. While more than a million of Hong

youtube_items.title: [YT] Inheritance of wealth and poverty rising in South Korea
youtube_items.summary: As South Korea struggles with a host of issues ranging from rapid population aging, shortages of good jobs for young people and sluggish overall growth, unfortunate trends like polarization of wealth are also grabbing attention. Recently, record high numbers of so-called silver spoon babies and teens have generated a lot of buzz while simultaneously people relying on government welfare have risen steadily. Reflecting such trends, the number of newborns receiving stock dividends surged nearly 10fold over the past 5 years. Many of them even have savings accounts to earn annual interest from stock payouts. summary: Неравенството и бедността продължават да бъдат сериозни предизвикателства, както във вътрешната политика на държави, така и в глобалния контекст. Съществуват усилия за анализиране и картографиране на бедността чрез иновативни технологии, но реалните проблеми остават неподвижни.
